RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF INVESTEK PROPERTIES COMPANY's Motion For Stay Of Enforcement is Denied. At most, the court would issue a stay to allow a hearing on the motion to correct. Such a stay would be improper because the motion to correct appears to be an unmeritorious motion for reconsideration. The court lacks jurisdiction to hear the motion to correct because the case was appealed. The court intends to stay this ruling for a period of 10 days to allow the plaintiff to file an application with the Court of Appeals for a stay order. RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MARK TECHNOLOGIES CORP'S Motion To Recall And Or Quash Writ Of Execution And Vacate Execution Of Levy And Liens is GRANTED. Judgment entered by this court on July 20, 2011, provides plaintiffs with their monetary damages through a constructive trust. It is clear from the language of the Judgment that the court is limiting the source of the recovery to the post-judgment royalties. The Court lists the gross amount of the judgment as $1,238,379.00, then states that the Defendants are entitled to a credit of $882,267.00, then states that the net of Plaintiff's judgment after accounting for the defendants' credit is $460,391 in addition to $72,342.00 in pre-judgment interest. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT MARK JONES' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted. As to the first cause of action, the Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") does not meet the pleading standard for alter ego liability set forth in Leek v. Cooper. ((2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 399.) The TAC does not state specific facts showing a unity of interest or that an inequitable result would result if alter ego liability was not imposed. As to the second cause of action, the TAC does not allege facts sufficient to establish that Defendant, as an individual, owed a fiduciary duty to Plaintiffs. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
